pi_items.source: Engineering specification; 5 kW is a standard residential/small turbine power rating
Engineering specification; represents ±1.0% tolerance (4950-5050 W range), which is reasonable for iterative design
Wikipedia: Tip-speed ratio; TSR 6 is confirmed as typical for three-bladed horizontal-axis wind turbines (HAWT)
Engineering standard; 10 m/s is a typical rated wind speed for small horizontal-axis wind turbines
Power calculation verification: At 2.2m radius, 10 m/s wind speed, and Cp=0.47 (typical for 3-bladed HAWT), power output = 4377 W, just within tolerance.
Power calculation verification: At 3.2m radius with same conditions, power output = 9261 W, representing upper bound of physically feasible sizing for 5 kW target.
Task specification; .wpa is the standard QBlade project file extension
Task specification; consistent with QBlade export format
Task specification; used for storing the final determined radius value

design_specification_claims.source: Airfoiltools.com confirms NACA 4412 is a real, well-documented airfoil with 4% camber at 40% chord, 12% thickness, widely used in wind turbine design
Engineering standard; 100 panels is a typical resolution for XFoil analysis of airfoils
XFoil documentation confirms Re=1e6 is within XFoil's valid range for aerodynamic analysis and is suitable for wind turbine blade elements at this scale
XFoil e^9 method documentation confirms NCrit=9 is the standard value for average wind tunnel conditions
Wind turbine design standard; three-bladed horizontal-axis rotors are the dominant commercial design
ScienceDirect research on VAWT design shows 0.25m chord is a reasonable specification; confirmed as within typical ranges for small turbine designs (0.05-0.8m range)
Engineering design choice; zero twist is a valid simplification for preliminary rotor design analysis

software_verification.claim: QBlade is used for BEM rotor simulation
software_verification.source: QBlade official documentation (qblade.org): QBlade is an open-source multi-physics wind turbine simulation software that includes BEM (Blade Element Momentum) analysis capabilities. Originally used BEM, now uses LLFVW as primary method with BEM as alternative.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'size_rotor_for_5kw_output' is a wind turbine iterative design exercise with verified technical specifications. Target output is 5000 W (±50 W tolerance, 4950-5050 W valid range) at 10 m/s wind speed with tip speed ratio of 6. Rotor radius bounds are plausible: 2.2 m minimum produces ~4377 W, 3.2 m maximum produces ~9261 W (verified via power calculations with typical power coefficient of 0.47). Design uses real NACA 4412 airfoil (4% camber, 12% thickness), analyzed via XFoil at Reynolds number 1×10^6 with standard NCrit=9 transition criterion. Rotor design is a simplified three-bladed horizontal-axis configuration with constant 0.25 m chord and zero twist. Simulation conducted in QBlade software with BEM method at specified conditions. All quantitative specifications are technically sound and consistent with standard wind turbine design practice. Task data is partially synthetic (user-generated simulation results from real software tools).
